steps.text: an agent submits a checkpoint_response with a very large state summary

the truncation decision (store vs. halt) is explicit and not based on JSON parse success alone

steps.text: the pipeline log file is unwritable due to disk-full or permission error
the heartbeat timer fires and the file write fails
the heartbeat banner is still printed to terminal stdout
the file write failure is NOT silently swallowed — the router increments its heartbeat_degraded counter for that failure class

on the first transition from healthy to degraded (counter goes from 0 to 1), the router emits a single "[WARN]" entry via the terminal output path identifying the failure class ("log-file-write") and the error message
subsequent ticks in the same degraded state increment the counter without emitting additional log entries (edge-triggered, not level-triggered)
when the file write succeeds again (counter returns to 0), the router emits a single "[INFO]" entry recording the recovery and the total count of failed ticks during the degraded interval
no mechanical error or halt is raised from log file write failure alone

scenarios.name: Heartbeat log file write failure increments a degraded counter and emits a single edge-triggered log entry

steps.text: terminal stdout has been closed or redirected to a non-writable target
the heartbeat timer fires and Console::Out write fails
the router increments its heartbeat_degraded counter for failure class "console-write"
on the first failure transition the router emits a single "[WARN]" entry to the log file path (which is still writable) identifying "console-write" as the failure class
the log file write still occurs if the file is writable
subsequent ticks in the same degraded state increment the counter without additional log entries (edge-triggered)
when console write recovers, a "[INFO]" recovery entry is emitted to the log file with the degraded tick count
no mechanical error or halt is raised from console write failure alone

scenarios.name: Heartbeat terminal write failure increments a degraded counter and emits a single edge-triggered log entry to the file

steps.text: the pipeline has been running with degraded heartbeat log writes for 7 years at the 10-second cadence (approximately 22 million ticks — approaching 2^24 but far below 2^31)
the heartbeat_degraded counter for failure class "log-file-write" has accumulated at least 1000 ticks
the pipeline halts (for any reason) and the user runs "./vibe.ps1 -Resume"
the router initializes all heartbeat_degraded counters to zero at the start of the resumed pipeline (counters do NOT persist across -Resume; they are in-process state)
the first heartbeat tick after -Resume starts from counter=0 for all failure classes

if the router is running continuously without -Resume: the heartbeat_degraded counter type is Int32 (or Int64) — if it approaches Int32.MaxValue (2,147,483,647), the router emits a "[WARN]" via the terminal path and saturates the counter at Int32.MaxValue rather than overflowing to a negative value

the saturation behavior is documented: at 1 failure per 10 seconds, saturation requires ≥ 680 years of continuous degraded operation; this is documented as the engineering justification for Int32 sufficiency

scenarios.name: heartbeat_degraded counter is reset on -Resume and bounded against integer overflow

steps.text: the Write-PipelineLog mutex is held by a concurrent event-log write for 8 seconds
the 10-second heartbeat timer fires while the mutex is held
the heartbeat tick is queued and fires immediately after the mutex is released
the delay is recorded in the heartbeat entry as an actual-vs-scheduled delta
the next heartbeat re-schedules from the actual fire time, not the scheduled time

scenarios.name: A slow Write-PipelineLog mutex does not cause the heartbeat to miss its 10-second tick

steps.text: the bus is processing 50 events per second (the design aggregate throughput) and Write-PipelineLog is called on every routed event
a test measures the per-acquisition mutex hold time for Write-PipelineLog over 1000 consecutive acquisitions
the p99 hold time per acquisition does not exceed 50 milliseconds (at 50 evt/s, a 50ms p99 hold queues at most 2.5 events behind one acquisition — acceptable)
the test fails if any single acquisition holds the mutex for more than 200 milliseconds (hard ceiling per acquisition — a 12-second hold that would queue 600 events is prohibited)
the p99 cap is committed to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json under key "write_pipeline_log_mutex_hold_p99_ms" as 50
the hard ceiling is committed as key "write_pipeline_log_mutex_hold_max_ms" as 200

the rate of hard-ceiling hits (acquisitions exceeding 200ms) does not exceed the bound committed under key "write_pipeline_log_ceiling_hit_rate_per_sec" — the bound is committed in performance-baselines.json as a maximum ceiling-hit rate per second (e.g., 0.1/sec = 1 in 500 acquisitions at 50 evt/s); the exact value is captured on first baseline run and enforced on subsequent runs
the CI job fails on either violation with separate failure messages distinguishing "p99 regression", "hard ceiling violation", and "ceiling-hit-rate exceeded"

"write_pipeline_log_ceiling_hit_rate_per_sec" is a measured baseline key, not a hardcoded constant — the rate is captured on the first baseline run and gated on subsequent runs (20% regression tolerance)

scenarios.name: Write-PipelineLog mutex hold time per acquisition is capped at p99 ≤ 50ms to prevent ConcurrentQueue backup under high event throughput

steps.text: the heartbeat timer last fired at system time T1
an NTP correction causes the system clock to jump backward so the current time is less than T1
the heartbeat timer fires and computes idle time for an alive agent
the idle-time calculation clamps any negative computed duration to zero
no exception or mechanical error is raised from the negative clock delta
the heartbeat banner displays "0s" for the affected agent's idle time rather than a negative or undefined value
the next heartbeat reschedules from the current adjusted system time, not from T1

scenarios.name: System clock jumps backward between heartbeat ticks due to NTP correction

steps.text: the heartbeat timer last fired at system time T1
an NTP correction causes the system clock to jump forward by 90 seconds so the computed idle time for all agents becomes inflated
the heartbeat timer fires and computes idle time for an alive agent whose last result event was at T1

steps.text: the mutation test runner is configured to target bus/router/ source files

the CI job reads the mutation operators exclusively from tests/bus/mutation-operators.md at the SHA256 hash committed to tests/bus/mutation-operators.md.sha256 (the hash file is committed to the repository and the CI job fails if the computed hash of mutation-operators.md does not match the committed hash — this prevents silent catalog drift)

the mutation operators enumerated in tests/bus/mutation-operators.md include at minimum: boundary off-by-one (e.g., replace ">=" with ">" in threshold comparisons), guard negation (e.g., negate if-condition guards on routing rules), constant replacement (e.g., swap a named status string "routed" for "committed"), routing-rule inversion (e.g., flip allowed/disallowed for a (role, type) pair), halt-condition inversion (e.g., change a mechanical-halt branch to a no-op), schema-field removal (e.g., remove a required field from a payload schema check), and allocator-boundary shift (e.g., change evt_id exhaustion check from MaxValue-1 to MaxValue)
the mutation test suite applies each enumerated operator to bus/router/ and runs tests/bus/properties/ against each mutant
at least 95% of injected mutants are killed by the property-test suite (project rule: "write tests, never add excludes" — 80% is insufficient)
surviving mutants are recorded in tests/bus/mutation-report.json with their location, applied operator name, and mutation description
the CI job fails if the kill rate drops below 95%

surviving mutants may only be documented as intentionally undetectable (and excluded from the kill-rate denominator) if they are structurally equivalent mutants (mutations that produce observationally identical behavior); escape-from-detection by weak tests is NOT grounds for exclusion

every documented known-gap surviving mutant in mutation-report.json must include a formal proof that the mutant is observationally equivalent — a comment like "test doesn't cover this" is not accepted
tests/bus/mutation-operators.md is the single source of truth for operator definitions; adding operators to the catalog (and updating the hash file) is required before they can be referenced in CI

scenarios.name: Mutation testing of router code measures property-test suite kill rate against a hash-pinned operator catalog

steps.text: a property test runs a simulated debate with a random objection/response/candidate sequence using the fixed seed stored under key "CandidateHasEventInLog" in tests/bus/properties/seeds.json
the router's internal consensusState reaches "candidate"

event_log contains at least one event with type="consensus_candidate" whose evt_id is greater than or equal to the consensus-round epoch marker (consensusRoundStart) — stale candidates from prior rollback rounds are excluded
the test verifies the epoch-scoped predicate: ∀ consensusState="candidate" ⇒ ∃ e ∈ eventLog : e.type="consensus_candidate" ∧ e.evt_id >= consensusRoundStart

the epoch used is consensusRoundStart (the consensus-round epoch, advanced on rollback), NOT spawnedAtEvt[a] (the agent-lifetime epoch, advanced on agent respawn) — the two epochs have distinct semantics: agent lifetime resets on respawn; consensus round resets on rollback
the test fails if consensusState="candidate" is observed without a scoped matching event_log entry with evt_id >= consensusRoundStart
on any failing sequence the runner shrinks to the minimal failing prefix and records the seed and prefix length in the test failure output

scenarios.name: CandidateHasEventInLog — every consensus_candidate event in the router's internal state has a matching record in event_log from within the current consensus epoch

steps.text: a property test exercises bus start, halt, resume, and semantic-termination cycles using the fixed seed stored under key "BusRunningImpliesLockHeld" in tests/bus/properties/seeds.json
the test samples busStatus at each transition
whenever busStatus is "running" or "resuming", pipeline_lock is TRUE in the SQLite pipeline_lock table
whenever busStatus transitions to "halted" due to semantic termination (consensus_ratified, consensus_failed, feature_complete), the pipeline_lock is subsequently released
the test fails if busStatus="running" is ever observed with pipeline_lock=FALSE
on any failing sequence the runner shrinks to the minimal failing prefix and records the seed and prefix length in the test failure output

scenarios.name: BusRunningImpliesLockHeld — the bus never enters "running" or "resuming" state while pipeline_lock is FALSE

steps.text: the bus is running (busStatus="running") and pipeline_lock is TRUE
an implementation defect or external process releases the pipeline_lock row while the router has not yet transitioned to "halted"
the router's lock-monitor detects that pipeline_lock is FALSE while busStatus="running"
the router halts with a mechanical error (failureCategory="sqlite_error" — the lock table is a SQLite concern)
Stop-AllBusAgents is called
the error message identifies "pipeline_lock released while bus running" as the cause
the process exits with exit code 14 (sqlite_error category per ExitCodeOf)
no further events are routed after the lock is detected as absent

scenarios.name: BusRunningImpliesLockHeld — bus halts with mechanical error if pipeline_lock is released while busStatus is still running

steps.text: a property test routes a random sequence of up to 500 events through the router using the fixed seed stored under key "MechanicalHaltHasCategory" in tests/bus/properties/seeds.json
the router halts and haltReason="mechanical_error" is observed in the final bus state

the state predicate busStatus="halted" ∧ haltReason="mechanical_error" ⇒ failureCategory ∈ FailureCategories holds across the entire property sequence
the test fails if any halted state with haltReason="mechanical_error" has failureCategory=null or failureCategory outside FailureCategories

the test verifies the bidirectional (v9 OBJ-15) direction: haltReason ≠ "mechanical_error" ⇒ failureCategory = null — semantic halts (haltReason ∈ {"consensus_ratified","consensus_failed","feature_complete","user_rollback"}) and user_interrupt (haltReason="user_interrupt") always have failureCategory=null

the test explicitly exercises the v10 post-resume-cycle case: a mechanical halt with non-null failureCategory followed by -Resume and a semantic halt must leave failureCategory=null at the semantic halt (FIX-1/2/3(v10))
on any failing sequence the runner shrinks to the minimal failing prefix and records the seed and prefix length in the test failure output

scenarios.name: MechanicalHaltHasCategory — whenever the bus halts with haltReason="mechanical_error", failureCategory is always a member of FailureCategories; and whenever haltReason is semantic, failureCategory is null

steps.text: a property test injects protocol_error events and checkpoint/crash sequences using the fixed seed stored under key "PendingProtocolErrorImpliesAgentAlive" in tests/bus/properties/seeds.json

the bus is running (BusRunning holds) and no snapshot-blocking condition is active
the test runs for sufficient router transitions for the WF condition to discharge
a UserRequestsRollback(wt1) action eventually fires (rollbackRequested transitions from FALSE to TRUE)
the test fails if UserRequestsRollback(wt1) never fires despite its enabling condition holding continuously (more than the configured liveness-check timeout)
on any failing sequence the runner shrinks to the minimal failing prefix and records the seed and prefix length in the test failure output

scenarios.name: WF_vars(UserRequestsRollback(w)) fairness — under enabling conditions, rollback request surfaces eventually

steps.text: a property test runs the router with no rollback pending, no halt condition, and snapshot-taking allowed for worktree "wt1" using the fixed seed stored under key "WF_RouterTakesSnapshot" in tests/bus/properties/seeds.json
the ~rollbackRequested guard (OBJ-3(v9)) is satisfied — rollbackRequested=FALSE
the test runs for sufficient router transitions for the WF condition to discharge
RouterTakesSnapshot(wt1) eventually fires (snapshotExists[wt1] transitions to TRUE)
the test fails if RouterTakesSnapshot(wt1) never fires despite its enabling condition holding continuously
on any failing sequence the runner shrinks to the minimal failing prefix and records the seed and prefix length in the test failure output

steps.text: a property test routes a random sequence of events covering all (role, type) pairs using the fixed seed stored under key "TypeSenderACL_from" in tests/bus/properties/seeds.json
every outbound envelope is validated by the router before appending to event_log
no event in event_log has a (from-role, type) pair that violates the TypeSenderACL rule table
for every rejected (role, type) pair, the router emits a protocol_error to the sender rather than a halt
specifically: a tla-writer constructing an envelope with type="consensus_ratified" is rejected before append because "consensus_ratified" is a router-only emit type
specifically: a handler (tlc, tests, git) constructing an envelope with type="done" is rejected because handlers may only emit "verify_result"
specifically: a debate-moderator constructing an envelope with type="bootstrap" is rejected because bootstrap is a router-to-agent type only
the test fails if any event_log row with status="routed" has a from field whose role is forbidden from emitting the event type
on any failing sequence the runner shrinks to the minimal failing prefix and records the seed and prefix length in the test failure output

scenarios.name: TypeSenderACL — no agent emits an event type forbidden for its role; ACL violations are rejected before append (from-side)

steps.text: a property test routes envelopes with type "<type>" addressed to "<invalid_recipient>" using the fixed seed stored under key "TypeSenderACL_to_<type>" in tests/bus/properties/seeds.json
the router validates the envelope's to-field against the TypeSenderACL recipient partition
the router rejects the envelope with a protocol_error identifying "<invalid_recipient>" as an invalid recipient for type "<type>"
no rejected envelope is appended to event_log with status="routed"
the pipeline does not halt — recipient ACL violations are protocol_error, not mechanical error
on any failing sequence the runner shrinks to the minimal failing prefix and records the seed and prefix length in the test failure output

examples.reason: review_requested addressed only to Agents or "broadcast"

scenarios.name: TypeSenderACL — recipient-side (to-side) constraints reject envelopes addressed to invalid targets (v9 OBJ-5)

steps.text: a property test routes a random sequence of verify events, verify_result events, and handler failures using the fixed seed stored under key "HandlerStateConsistency" in tests/bus/properties/seeds.json
the test samples handlerState, handlerPendingAgent, handlerPendingEvt, and handlerPendingEpoch for each handler at every router transition

for every handler h: (handlerPendingAgent[h] = null) = (handlerPendingEvt[h] = null) = (handlerPendingEpoch[h] = null) holds at every transition (tri-field biconditional — v9 OBJ-9 extended to include epoch)
whenever handlerState[h]="idle", all three fields (handlerPendingAgent[h], handlerPendingEvt[h], handlerPendingEpoch[h]) are null
whenever handlerState[h]="busy", all three fields are non-null
when an agent crashes mid-handler-invocation (AgentCrashes fires with handlerPendingAgent[h]=a), all three fields are atomically cleared to null and handlerState[h] returns to "idle"
the test fails if any transition leaves any one of the three fields non-null while the others are null
on any failing sequence the runner shrinks to the minimal failing prefix and records the seed and prefix length in the test failure output

scenarios.name: HandlerStateConsistency — handler request fields idle when handler is idle, all populated when busy (v9 tri-field biconditional)

steps.text: agent "tla-writer" was alive with spawnedAtEvt="evt-010" and sent a verify event to handler "tlc"
handlerPendingAgent["tlc"]="tla-writer" and handlerPendingEpoch["tlc"]="evt-010" were recorded at dispatch time
"tla-writer" crashed before the TLC invocation completed
-Resume re-spawned "tla-writer" as a new process with spawnedAtEvt="evt-025" (new agent-lifetime epoch)
the TLC invocation completes and HandlerAdapterCompletes fires with handlerPendingEpoch["tlc"]="evt-010"
the router checks spawnedAtEvt for the current "tla-writer" agent ("evt-025") against handlerPendingEpoch["tlc"] ("evt-010")
the epoch guard rejects the completion because spawnedAtEvt[a] ("evt-025") ≠ handlerPendingEpoch[h] ("evt-010")
the stale verify_result is NOT delivered to the re-spawned "tla-writer"
the handler state for "tlc" is cleared atomically: handlerPendingAgent["tlc"]=null, handlerPendingEvt["tlc"]=null, handlerPendingEpoch["tlc"]=null, handlerState["tlc"]="idle"
the router halts with a mechanical error (epoch mismatch indicates a dangling handler request from a prior agent lifetime)
Stop-AllBusAgents is called

scenarios.name: Handler epoch mismatch — HandlerAdapterCompletes rejects a stale completion after agent respawn

steps.text: a property test exercises rollback sequences with and without prior snapshots using the fixed seed stored under key "RollbackRequiresSnapshot" in tests/bus/properties/seeds.json
the test observes rollbackRequested=TRUE in the router state
RouterExecutesRollback fires only if snapshotExists[wt]=TRUE for the worktree associated with the rollback target (worktree-scoped pre-condition)
if no snapshot exists for the target worktree, the rollback attempt is rejected with a defined error rather than silently proceeding on a snapshot from a different worktree
the test verifies the invariant ∀ state: rollbackRequested=TRUE ∧ snapshotExists[targetWorktree]=FALSE ⇒ RouterExecutesRollback does not fire
the test fails if rollback fires without a matching worktree-scoped snapshot
on any failing sequence the runner shrinks to the minimal failing prefix and records the seed and prefix length in the test failure output

scenarios.name: RollbackRequiresSnapshot — a rollback can only be executed when at least one snapshot exists for the target worktree

steps.text: a property test requests a rollback (rollbackRequested=TRUE) while a valid worktree-scoped snapshot exists using the fixed seed stored under key "RollbackEventuallyCompletes" in tests/bus/properties/seeds.json
fairness assumption WF_vars(RouterExecutesRollback) is active — the router is not blocked by a live mechanical error or concurrent in-flight commit
the test runs for sufficient router transitions for the WF condition to discharge
rollbackRequested eventually becomes FALSE (rollback completed) or busStatus becomes "halted" from an unrelated mechanical error
the test fails if rollbackRequested remains TRUE indefinitely without completion or halt (more than the configured liveness-check timeout)
the test explicitly verifies that a pending commit (commitLockHolder[wt] non-null) does not permanently block rollback — the rollback proceeds once the commit completes
on any failing sequence the runner shrinks to the minimal failing prefix and records the seed and prefix length in the test failure output

scenarios.name: RollbackEventuallyCompletes liveness — once rollback is requested, it eventually completes or the bus halts for an unrelated mechanical reason

steps.text: a property test routes a random sequence of verify events, agent crashes, and handler completions using the fixed seed stored under key "NoOrphanedHandlerForDeadAgent" in tests/bus/properties/seeds.json
the test observes agentStatus[a]="dead" for any agent a in the router state
for every handler h in Handlers: handlerPendingAgent[h] ≠ a — no handler retains a pending-agent reference to the dead agent
the state predicate agentStatus[a]="dead" ⇒ ∀ h ∈ Handlers : handlerPendingAgent[h] ≠ a holds at every transition

the test verifies that AgentCrashes atomically clears handlerPendingAgent[h]=null, handlerPendingEvt[h]=null, handlerPendingEpoch[h]=null for any handler h where handlerPendingAgent[h]=a at the time of crash
the test fails if any handler's handlerPendingAgent field references an agent with status="dead"
on any failing sequence the runner shrinks to the minimal failing prefix and records the seed and prefix length in the test failure output

scenarios.name: NoOrphanedHandlerForDeadAgent — no handler remains pending for a dead agent

steps.text: the bus is running (busStatus="running") with rollbackRequested=TRUE and rollbackTargetWorktree="wt-auth-flow"
consensusState="open", unresolvedObjections={"evt-031"}, overriddenObjections=∅ (pre-halt consensus state)
a SQLite error occurs during the rollback execution sequence (e.g., locked database or constraint violation mid-rollback)
RouterHaltsRollbackSqliteError fires
the router halts with haltReason="mechanical_error" and failureCategory="sqlite_error"
rollbackRequested is cleared to FALSE
rollbackTargetWorktree is cleared to NULL
the ConsensusRound aggregate IS reset atomically: consensusState="open", unresolvedObjections=∅, overriddenObjections=∅ (v11 OBJ-EC-3 symmetric clean-slate principle)
consensusRoundStart is advanced to the current nextEvtId value (v11 OBJ-A — prevents pre-halt objections in the immutable eventLog from satisfying post-resume ModeratorEmitsCandidate guards)
Stop-AllBusAgents is called
the process exits with exit code 14 (sqlite_error per ExitCodeOf)
event_log is NOT mutated (the rollback halt does not append any new event type to event_log)
a subsequent "./vibe.ps1 -Resume" is available to the user, who may retry "/rollback" after resolving the SQLite issue

scenarios.name: RouterHaltsRollbackSqliteError — SQLite error during rollback execution halts the bus with sqlite_error and resets the ConsensusRound aggregate

steps.text: the pipeline halted via RouterHaltsRollbackSqliteError with failureCategory="sqlite_error"
agent_sessions shows all agents with status="ended"
bus_snapshots still contains the target snapshot (rollback was not applied)
the user runs "./vibe.ps1 -Resume"
the router re-spawns agents from the pre-rollback SQLite state (bootstrap + ground_truth)
the bus enters busStatus="running" with rollbackRequested=FALSE and rollbackTargetWorktree=NULL
the user may issue a fresh "./vibe.ps1 -Rollback -SnapId <snap_id>" after confirming the SQLite issue is resolved
event_log is intact and unmodified by the failed rollback attempt

scenarios.name: -Resume after RouterHaltsRollbackSqliteError allows retrying /rollback

steps.text: the bus is running (busStatus="running") with rollbackRequested=TRUE and rollbackTargetWorktree="wt-auth-flow"
snapshotExists["wt-auth-flow"]=TRUE (the target snapshot is present)
consensusState="open", unresolvedObjections={"evt-031"} (a debate is in flight)
the user presses Ctrl+C (UserInterrupts fires) while rollbackRequested=TRUE is still pending
the bus halts with haltReason="user_interrupt" and failureCategory=NULL (UserInterrupts semantics)
rollbackRequested remains TRUE immediately after the Ctrl+C halt (the halt precedes the abort)
RouterAbortsStaleRollback subsequently fires (WF_vars guarantees eventual firing once busStatus="halted" AND rollbackRequested=TRUE)
RouterAbortsStaleRollback clears rollbackRequested to FALSE and rollbackTargetWorktree to NULL
snapshotExists["wt-auth-flow"] remains TRUE — the snapshot is NOT consumed (rollback did not execute)
consensusState, unresolvedObjections, and overriddenObjections are NOT reset (RouterAbortsStaleRollback does NOT advance consensusRoundStart — no clean-slate boundary occurred)
the user may run "./vibe.ps1 -Resume" followed by a fresh "./vibe.ps1 -Rollback -SnapId <snap_id>" to retry the rollback

scenarios.name: RouterAbortsStaleRollback — Ctrl+C during pending rollback aborts the stale rollback and preserves the snapshot

steps.text: the bus has halted with haltReason="mechanical_error" and failureCategory="agent_crash" (busStatus="halted")
snapshotExists["wt-auth-flow"]=TRUE
the user invokes "./vibe.ps1 -Rollback -SnapId snap-003" while the bus is already halted
UserRequestsRollback fires and sets rollbackRequested=TRUE (the TLA+ action accepts busStatus="halted")
RouterExecutesRollback does NOT fire because BusRunning=FALSE (BusRunning guard not satisfied)
RouterAbortsStaleRollback fires (busStatus="halted" AND rollbackRequested=TRUE is satisfied)
RouterAbortsStaleRollback clears rollbackRequested to FALSE and rollbackTargetWorktree to NULL
snapshotExists["wt-auth-flow"] remains TRUE — the snapshot is preserved for the retry
busStatus, haltReason, and failureCategory remain unchanged (the abort does not overwrite the existing halt state)
the CLI reports user-visible feedback: "Rollback aborted: bus is halted. Run './vibe.ps1 -Resume' first, then re-issue '/rollback' after the bus is running."
the process exits with the original halt exit code (exit 15 for agent_crash, unchanged by the abort)

scenarios.name: RouterAbortsStaleRollback — /rollback issued against a halted bus is auto-aborted with user-visible feedback

steps.text: a property test places the bus in busStatus="halted" with rollbackRequested=TRUE using the fixed seed stored under key "RouterAbortsStaleRollback" in tests/bus/properties/seeds.json
WF_vars(RouterAbortsStaleRollback) fairness is active — once continuously enabled, the action must eventually fire
the test runs for sufficient router transitions for the WF condition to discharge
rollbackRequested eventually becomes FALSE (RouterAbortsStaleRollback fired)
the test fails if rollbackRequested remains TRUE indefinitely while busStatus="halted" (more than the configured liveness-check timeout)
the test verifies that UserResumes is unblocked after the abort (rollbackRequested=FALSE satisfies UserResumes precondition)
the test verifies both entry paths: (a) UserInterrupts-then-RouterAbortsStaleRollback and (b) UserRequestsRollback(halted)-then-RouterAbortsStaleRollback
on any failing sequence the runner shrinks to the minimal failing prefix and records the seed and prefix length in the test failure output

scenarios.name: RouterAbortsStaleRollback WF fairness liveness — stale rollback is always eventually cleared when bus is halted

steps.text: the bus halted with haltReason="mechanical_error" and failureCategory="agent_crash" due to an agent process crash
the pipeline_log records the halt with exit code 15
the user runs "./vibe.ps1 -Resume" and the bus re-enters busStatus="running"
the resumed bus reaches consensus_ratified (a semantic termination — RouterRatifiesConsensus fires)
haltReason="consensus_ratified" and failureCategory=NULL in the final bus state
the process exits with exit code 0

the test verifies this specific trace order: mechanical_error halt (failureCategory="agent_crash") → -Resume → BusRunning → RouterRatifiesConsensus → halted(haltReason="consensus_ratified", failureCategory=NULL)
the test does NOT rely on random event ordering — the trace is explicitly constructed to force this sequence
the same assertion applies to RouterHaltsFeatureComplete (FIX-1) and RouterFailsConsensus (FIX-3): all three semantic halt actions must set failureCategory=NULL explicitly

scenarios.name: v10 FIX-1/2/3 — failureCategory is null at semantic halt after a mechanical-halt-then-resume cycle

steps.text: a debate has agents "expert-tla" and "expert-bdd" with unresolved objections evt-031 and evt-032 respectively
"expert-tla" has groundTruthDelivered=TRUE (ground truth was prepended to the last message in this round)
"expert-bdd" has groundTruthDelivered=FALSE (ground truth has not yet been delivered in this round)
the debate moderator attempts to emit a consensus_candidate event
the router blocks the consensus_candidate from being routed because groundTruthDelivered["expert-bdd"]=FALSE while evt-032 remains unresolved (OBJ-10(v9) guard)
the router delivers ground_truth to "expert-bdd" before allowing the consensus_candidate to proceed
only after groundTruthDelivered["expert-bdd"] transitions to TRUE does the router route the consensus_candidate
event_log confirms the ordering: ground_truth to "expert-bdd" → consensus_candidate from moderator
the test verifies that the ground-truth-per-objector guard applies to all agents with current-round unresolved objections, not just the moderator

scenarios.name: ModeratorEmitsCandidate ground-truth guard — consensus_candidate is not emitted until every agent with an unresolved objection has received ground_truth in the current round

steps.text: a debate produced objection evt-031 from "expert-tla" (evt_id=31) and objection evt-032 from "expert-bdd" (evt_id=32)

the user invokes "/rollback" and RouterExecutesRollback fires, resetting consensusState="open", unresolvedObjections=∅, overriddenObjections=∅, and advancing consensusRoundStart to the current nextEvtId (e.g., consensusRoundStart=50)
the bus is resumed and agents are respawned for the post-rollback round
the debate moderator attempts to emit a consensus_candidate event in the fresh round (no new objections have been raised yet)
the ModeratorEmitsCandidate guard evaluates objections as: ∃ e ∈ eventLog : e.type="objection" ∧ e.evt_id >= consensusRoundStart (50)
evt-031 (evt_id=31) and evt-032 (evt_id=32) do NOT satisfy this guard because 31 < 50 and 32 < 50
no consensus_candidate is blocked by the pre-rollback objections in the immutable eventLog
the router routes the consensus_candidate without requiring ground_truth delivery for "expert-tla" or "expert-bdd" based on pre-rollback objections
event_log confirms the fresh round proceeds independently of the pre-rollback debate history

scenarios.name: consensusRoundStart epoch — post-rollback fresh consensus round ignores pre-rollback objections in eventLog

steps.text: a debate produced objections evt-041 and evt-042 (evt_ids 41, 42) before a SQLite error interrupted the rollback
RouterHaltsRollbackSqliteError fired, resetting the ConsensusRound aggregate and advancing consensusRoundStart to nextEvtId (e.g., consensusRoundStart=60)
the user resolves the SQLite issue and runs "./vibe.ps1 -Resume"
agents are respawned and the fresh post-resume debate begins without any new objections yet
the ModeratorEmitsCandidate guard uses consensusRoundStart=60 as the epoch filter
evt-041 (evt_id=41) and evt-042 (evt_id=42) do NOT satisfy the ∃ guard (41 < 60, 42 < 60)
the moderator may emit a consensus_candidate without being blocked by the pre-halt objections
the test distinguishes RouterHaltsRollbackSqliteError (consensusRoundStart advances) from RouterAbortsStaleRollback (consensusRoundStart does NOT advance, no clean-slate boundary)

scenarios.name: RouterHaltsRollbackSqliteError advances consensusRoundStart — post-resume round is isolated from pre-halt objections

steps.text: a debate produced objection evt-031 from "expert-tla" (evt_id=31) with groundTruthDelivered["expert-tla"]=FALSE at the time of rollback
RouterExecutesRollback fired, advancing consensusRoundStart to 50, resetting unresolvedObjections=∅
the bus is resumed; "expert-tla" is respawned with groundTruthDelivered["expert-tla"] reset to FALSE (RouterRespawnsAgent resets this flag)
the debate moderator attempts to emit a consensus_candidate in the fresh post-rollback round (no new objections raised yet)
the ModeratorEmitsCandidate guard evaluates: ∀ e ∈ eventLog : (e.type="objection" ∧ e.from ∈ Agents ∧ e.evt_id >= consensusRoundStart=50) ⇒ groundTruthDelivered[e.from]
evt-031 (evt_id=31) does NOT satisfy e.evt_id >= 50, so "expert-tla"'s groundTruthDelivered state is irrelevant to the post-rollback guard
the consensus_candidate is NOT blocked by the pre-rollback objector's groundTruthDelivered=FALSE state
event_log confirms the moderator emits consensus_candidate without waiting for ground_truth re-delivery to "expert-tla" based on the pre-rollback objection

scenarios.name: OBJ-B coupling — pre-rollback objector with groundTruthDelivered=FALSE does not block fresh post-rollback consensus round

steps.text: a debate has two eras of objections in the immutable eventLog:
pre-rollback objections evt-015 and evt-016 (evt_ids 15, 16) from a prior round
post-rollback objections evt-071 and evt-072 (evt_ids 71, 72) from the current round (consensusRoundStart=65)
groundTruthDelivered["expert-tla"]=TRUE and groundTruthDelivered["expert-bdd"]=TRUE for the current round
the ModeratorEmitsCandidate guard is evaluated
the ∃ guard is satisfied by evt-071 and evt-072 (71 >= 65, 72 >= 65 — current-round objections)
the ∀ ground-truth guard applies only to agents whose objection evt_id >= consensusRoundStart=65 (expert-tla and expert-bdd for evt-071, evt-072)
the router emits consensus_candidate (both guards satisfied by current-round data alone)
the test asserts: if only pre-rollback objections evt-015, evt-016 existed (no post-rollback objections), the ∃ guard would FAIL (15 < 65, 16 < 65) and no consensus_candidate would be emitted

scenarios.name: consensusRoundStart epoch — consensus_candidate event must NOT be emitted using objection events whose evt_id is below consensusRoundStart

features.name: TLA+ safety invariants are verified at runtime as property-based tests

steps.text: a trace file is captured from a real or simulated pipeline run
the trace file format is validated
each line is a valid JSON object with fields: evt_id, from, to, in_reply_to, type, payload, group_id (nullable), status, captured_at
the file uses UTF-8 encoding with LF line endings
the evt_ids within the file are unique and monotonically increasing

scenarios.name: A trace file is valid NDJSON with one event envelope per line

steps.text: a test or development run is started with the VIBE_CAPTURE_TRACE environment variable set
events are routed through the bus
each routed event is appended to a trace file in tests/bus/traces/ named "<feature>-<timestamp>.ndjson"
capture does not affect routing behavior or event_log contents

scenarios.name: Trace capture is triggered by a test flag and writes to tests/bus/traces/

steps.text: a trace file "auth-flow-2026-04-17.ndjson" exists in tests/bus/traces/
claude-test-double.ps1 and git-test-double.ps1 are configured to respond to the trace's events
the trace replay runner is invoked with the trace file
the router processes each event from the trace in order
the router does not require real agent processes to replay

scenarios.name: Trace replay drives the bus through the captured sequence using test doubles

steps.text: the trace replay runner has completed processing "auth-flow-2026-04-17.ndjson"
the oracle compares the replay event_log to the captured trace
every evt_id in the trace appears in the replay event_log with the same type, from, and to fields
the oracle compares payload semantics using canonical JSON form: both the captured payload and the replay payload are normalized to sorted keys and no insignificant whitespace before comparison
a payload that differs after canonical normalization is a test failure; a payload that differs only in whitespace or key ordering is NOT a test failure
timing deltas are NOT asserted: the oracle does not compare captured_at timestamps or inter-event durations between the trace and the replay
the oracle reports a test failure if any evt_id is missing, has a different type, has a different routing target, or has a payload that differs after canonical normalization
the canonical normalization function is the same shared implementation used in both capture and replay paths to prevent divergence

scenarios.name: Trace replay oracle validates event_log outcome matches the captured trace using canonical JSON comparison

steps.text: a pipeline crashed mid-run and a trace was captured up to the crash point
the trace is replayed in the test environment
the bus reaches the same final event_log state as the captured crash
the crash-triggering condition (e.g., duplicate evt_id, group violation) is reproduced and observable in the replay

scenarios.name: A trace captured at the moment of a crash enables exact crash reproduction

features.name: Trace replay allows capturing and re-running event sequences as deterministic regression tests

steps.text: the CI pipeline is building a PR or merging to main
the performance-test CI job starts
a pre-check step asserts that tests/bus/performance-baselines.json exists in the repository at the current HEAD
the CI job fails immediately with the message "tests/bus/performance-baselines.json not found — commit the baselines file before enabling performance tests" if the file is absent
no performance benchmark is measured or compared until the file exists
this gate prevents CI from silently comparing against a missing baseline and reporting a false pass

scenarios.name: performance-baselines.json is committed to the repository before any performance test runs

steps.text: tests/bus/performance-baselines.json does not yet exist in the repository (first-time capture)
the CI job named "capture-baselines" is invoked explicitly (not the standard "run-performance-tests" job)
the capture job runs with assertions DISABLED (no regression gates fire)

it measures observed values for all performance metrics: throughput (evt/s), p50 latency (ms), p95 latency (ms), p99 latency (ms), per_agent_rss_mb_max, total_rss_mb_max, wal_size_mb_max, wal_checkpoint_interval_events, in_reply_to_chain_max_depth, fd_budget_max, soak_max_event_log_rows, and checkpoint_wall_clock_ms
it writes the observed values to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json in the repository, including the runner_spec object (cpu_class, ram_gb, disk_type)
the capture job commits and pushes the baselines file with a commit message identifying it as a baseline capture run
once the baselines file is committed, subsequent "run-performance-tests" jobs use it for regression gating
the capture job is documented to run exactly once per new CI runner class; re-running it intentionally overwrites the prior baselines

scenarios.name: Baseline-capture mode seeds tests/bus/performance-baselines.json without gating on the file's prior existence

steps.text: a performance test routes events continuously through the router with in-memory SQLite for a minimum of 30 seconds
the test measures total elapsed time and total events routed
the aggregate throughput is at least 50 events per second over the full 30-second window
no mechanical errors occur during the run

scenarios.name: Router sustains at least 50 events per second under sustained load for at least 30 seconds

steps.text: a performance test measures the time from Send-BusEvent call to event_log row insertion for 500 events (scope: per-message latency, not aggregate or e2e)
latency percentiles are computed
the p50 latency (per-message scope) is below 500 milliseconds
the p95 latency (per-message scope) is below 1200 milliseconds
the p99 latency (per-message scope) is below 2000 milliseconds
the p95 metric is reported independently so tail-latency regressions in the 20%-above-p95 band are detectable without relying on p99 alone

scenarios.name: End-to-end per-message event latency is within bounds at all percentiles including p95

steps.text: event_log contains 10000 rows with mixed status, type, and group_id values
the event_log has a composite index on (status, type, group_id)
the heartbeat query runs to compute idle times for alive agents
the query completes in under 200 milliseconds
the index presence is verified as part of the migration schema test

scenarios.name: Heartbeat query completes in under 200 milliseconds at 10000 event_log rows

steps.text: Stage 7 has more tasks to dispatch than the configured process ceiling (default: 16 simultaneous agents)
the pipeline dispatches tasks
the number of alive agent_sessions rows never exceeds the ceiling at any point
excess tasks queue until a slot is available
the ceiling is configurable via vibe configuration, not hardcoded in the router

scenarios.name: Stage 7 concurrent agent count does not exceed the configured process ceiling

steps.text: a performance baseline captures the token cost of starting a fresh agent with bootstrap + ground_truth
a renewal sequence captures the token cost of bootstrap + ground_truth + checkpoint_response
the costs are compared for an agent that has processed 40 turns
the renewal path consumes at least 20% fewer tokens than the equivalent cold-start path would require to reconstruct the same effective context
the test fails if the measured savings are less than 20%
the savings percentage is logged as a pipeline metric for tracking over time

scenarios.name: Session renewal costs at least 20 percent fewer tokens than a cold-start equivalent for the same agent role

steps.text: all performance tests run in a dedicated CI environment with no noisy-neighbor processes sharing CPU or I/O
each performance metric is preceded by 3 warm-up laps before measurement begins
a performance test records a throughput result
the result is compared against the committed baseline at tests/bus/performance-baselines.json
the test fails if throughput regresses by more than 10% versus the baseline value
the baseline file must be updated explicitly and committed when performance intentionally changes (accidental drift is a test failure)

scenarios.name: Throughput regression is detected independently as a dedicated CI failure

steps.text: the same CI environment and warm-up configuration as the throughput test
a performance test records p50, p95, and p99 latency results (per-message scope)
the test fails if p50 latency increases by more than 20% versus the p50 baseline value
the test fails if p95 latency increases by more than 20% versus the p95 baseline value
the test fails if p99 latency increases by more than 20% versus the p99 baseline value
p95 baseline is committed to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json under key "p95_latency_ms"
a latency regression is reported as a separate CI failure from a throughput regression
p50, p95, and p99 are each reported as individual CI annotations so regressions are pinpointed without reading combined output

scenarios.name: Latency regression is detected independently as a dedicated CI failure across all three percentiles

steps.text: the same CI environment and warm-up configuration as the throughput test
a performance test records the session-renewal token-savings percentage
the test fails if token savings drop by more than 5 percentage points versus the baseline value
a token-savings regression is reported as a separate CI failure from throughput and latency regressions

scenarios.name: Token-savings regression is detected independently as a dedicated CI failure

steps.text: tests/bus/performance-baselines.json is committed to the repository
the file is inspected
it contains a "runner_spec" object with at minimum: cpu_class (e.g., "2-core x86_64"), ram_gb (e.g., 7), disk_type (e.g., "SSD")
the CI environment that measures performance must match the runner_spec before recording results
a CI job fails if a PR modifies the numeric baseline thresholds without also updating the runner_spec or adding a "change_rationale" field explaining the intentional change
this provenance check prevents silent baseline edits that would bypass regression enforcement

scenarios.name: Performance baselines file includes CI runner specification for provenance

steps.text: Stage 7 has exactly the process ceiling (default: 16) alive agents in agent_sessions
the router determines that one of those agents requires checkpoint renewal
the router does not call Start-BusAgent for the replacement until Stop-BusAgent has fully returned for the old agent
at no point during the renewal sequence does agent_sessions contain more than ceiling (16) rows with status="alive"
the transient alive-count during checkpoint respawn is strictly bounded by ceiling, not ceiling+1

scenarios.name: Checkpoint-driven respawn does not transiently overshoot the configured process ceiling

steps.text: a soak test runs the router continuously for 60 minutes with in-memory SQLite and 16 concurrent agents simulated via test doubles
the test completes
no individual agent process exceeds the per-agent RSS bound committed to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json (key: "per_agent_rss_mb_max")
the SQLite WAL file size does not exceed the WAL bound committed to performance-baselines.json (key: "wal_size_mb_max") at any 10-minute checkpoint
the router performs a PRAGMA wal_checkpoint at the interval committed to performance-baselines.json (key: "wal_checkpoint_interval_events") to prevent unbounded WAL growth
no mechanical errors occur during the 60-minute run
the per-message p50 latency measured in the final 10-minute window does not exceed 125% of the p50 latency measured in the first 10-minute window (latency drift ≤ 25%)
the per-message p95 latency measured in the final 10-minute window does not exceed 125% of the p95 latency measured in the first 10-minute window
the per-message p99 latency measured in the final 10-minute window does not exceed 125% of the p99 latency measured in the first 10-minute window
the aggregate throughput measured in the final 10-minute window does not fall below 80% of the throughput measured in the first 10-minute window (throughput drift ≤ 20%)
the event_log row count at the end of the soak does not exceed the bound committed to performance-baselines.json (key: "soak_max_event_log_rows") ensuring no unbounded log growth
GC/allocator growth is bounded: the PowerShell process working set at the 60-minute mark does not exceed 150% of the working set at the 1-minute mark
these drift assertions are evaluated at 10-minute intervals throughout the run so a router that stops routing mid-soak will fail at the first interval where throughput drops
the soak test fails if ANY of the above bounds are breached at ANY measurement interval

scenarios.name: 60-minute sustained soak test does not exceed per-agent RSS, WAL growth, latency drift, or throughput drift bounds

steps.text: a test spawns 16 concurrent claude-test-double.ps1 processes via Start-BusAgent
each test double runs for at least 5 minutes exchanging events
the test measures the working-set (RSS) of each process at 1-minute intervals
the peak RSS for each individual agent process does not exceed the bound in tests/bus/performance-baselines.json (key: "per_agent_rss_mb_max")
the aggregate RSS of all 16 agent processes does not exceed the aggregate bound in performance-baselines.json (key: "total_rss_mb_max")

scenarios.name: Per-agent RSS envelope is measured and bounded for 16 concurrent agents

steps.text: a test spawns 16 agents, each with 3 pipes (stdin, stdout, stderr), plus 1 SQLite connection, 1 log file handle, and 1 git handle per worktree (8 worktrees)
all handles are open simultaneously and 500 events are routed
no EMFILE (too many open files) error is raised
the test asserts the total handle count is within the configurable file-descriptor budget in tests/bus/performance-baselines.json (key: "fd_budget_max")
if the handle count approaches within 20% of the OS limit the test logs an "[ALARM]" warning via Write-PipelineLog

scenarios.name: File descriptor exhaustion is tested for 16 agents with all pipes and handles open simultaneously

steps.text: the router is processing 50 events per second (aggregate throughput scope) across 16 alive agents
ground-truth composition is measured across a 30-second window
the aggregate ground-truth composition time per event (summed across all 16 per-agent compositions triggered by that event, aggregate scope) does not exceed 50 milliseconds

the per-agent ground-truth composition mean is 3.1 milliseconds (derived: 50ms ÷ 16 agents — this is the MEAN, not a per-agent ceiling; the metric key in performance-baselines.json is "ground_truth_composition_mean_ms_per_agent" to prevent misreading as a ceiling)
if the aggregate budget is exceeded for any single event, Write-PipelineLog records a warning identifying the triggering event, the total composition time, and which agent roles were slowest
the ground-truth composition does not perform unbounded SQLite scans: it queries only indexed columns with a row limit of at most 2× the current alive agent count
the ground-truth query plan is verified via EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN: no full-table scan (no "SCAN TABLE event_log" without an index) is produced for the ground-truth fetch

the per-turn SQLite read count per agent for ground-truth composition does not exceed 2 reads per alive agent per ground-truth cycle (cold-cache and warm-cache bounds are separately committed to performance-baselines.json)

scenarios.name: Aggregate ground-truth composition budget does not exceed 50ms per event at 50 evt/s with 16 agents (aggregate scope, not per-message)

steps.text: event_log contains 10000 rows representing approximately 3.3 hours of pipeline activity at 50 evt/s
the heartbeat fires every 10 seconds (360 times per hour)

the heartbeat emitter runspace executes 360 consecutive queries against the 10000-row event_log (emitter-side cost only; idle-time listener polling cost is budgeted separately under key "heartbeat_listener_poll_p95_ms" in performance-baselines.json)

the CPU time consumed by all 360 heartbeat emitter queries combined does not exceed 72 CPU-seconds per hour (200ms p95 per-query cap × 360 queries = 72 s/hr at p95; budget is derived from the p95 cap, not the p50)
the p50 CPU time for all 360 queries combined is at most 18 CPU-seconds per hour (50ms p50 × 360)
each individual heartbeat query completes in under 200 milliseconds at p95 as established in the heartbeat-query performance scenario
the heartbeat query uses only indexed columns (verified in the migration schema test) to prevent full-table scans
this bound is committed to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json under key "heartbeat_max_cpu_sec_per_hr" as 72 (p95 budget, not p50)

scenarios.name: Heartbeat emitter CPU cost is bounded for a 10000-row event_log — emitter-side only, no double-count with listener

steps.text: the heartbeat listener polls for idle-time data to display in the heartbeat banner
the listener poll fires at the same 10-second cadence as the emitter
360 consecutive listener poll calls are measured against a 10000-row event_log (3.3 hours of activity)
the p95 poll latency is observed and committed to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json under key "heartbeat_listener_poll_p95_ms"
this scenario is @observational-only until the baseline-capture CI job has committed the initial value — no regression gate fires on first capture
once a baseline value is committed, subsequent CI runs gate: p95 listener poll must not regress by more than 20% versus the committed baseline
the listener poll uses only indexed columns (verified in migration schema test) to prevent full-table scans

scenarios.name: Heartbeat listener poll cost — p95 observed and committed; not gated until baseline-capture run confirms value

steps.text: 16 agents are simultaneously sending done events triggering evt_id allocation
the allocator is called 160 times in a burst (16 agents × 10 events each) within a single 1-second window
all 160 allocations complete within the 1-second window
the allocator throughput is at least 500 allocations/second as committed to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json under key "evt_id_allocator_min_allocs_per_sec"

500 allocations/second represents ≥ 10× the design aggregate event rate of 50 evt/s — headroom is at least 10× aggregate load (documented in performance-baselines.json under key "evt_id_allocator_headroom_factor" as 10)
at 16 callers the per-caller throughput floor is ≥ 31 allocations/second (500 ÷ 16 = 31.25/caller) — verified in the contended-ratio assertion below
the synchronization primitive protecting the allocator does not become the throughput ceiling at 16-agent fan-out
the test measures allocator throughput in isolation (no SQLite writes) and under full load (with concurrent SQLite writes) and asserts the ratio is ≥ 0.80

scenarios.name: evt_id allocator throughput does not become a bottleneck under 16-agent fan-out

steps.text: the router is configured with groups of varying sizes: 1, 4, 8, and 16 members
Wait-BusGroup completes for each group size after all members have replied simultaneously
the p50 aggregation latency for 1 member is under 5 milliseconds
the p50 aggregation latency for 4 members is under 20 milliseconds
the p50 aggregation latency for 8 members is under 40 milliseconds
the p50 aggregation latency for 16 members is under 80 milliseconds

the test fails if any group size produces super-linear aggregation growth: specifically, 16 members must not take more than 4.0× the p50 latency of 4 members (named slack: exact multiple 4.0, not 3.9)
the test additionally asserts: 8-member p50 latency ≤ 2.0× 4-member p50 latency (named 8-vs-4 bound — a 3.9× quadratic creep from 4→8 is also a failure, not just 4→16)
these two scaling assertions are independently reportable: a super-linear jump from 4 to 8 members is a failure even if the 16-member assertion passes
the bounds are committed to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json under key "fan_out_aggregation_latency_ms" indexed by group size

scenarios.name: Fan-out aggregation latency scales linearly as group size grows from 1 to 16 members

steps.text: an agent undergoes a full checkpoint renewal cycle (checkpoint event → checkpoint_response → Stop-BusAgent → Start-BusAgent → deliver bootstrap + ground_truth + checkpoint_response)
the full renewal cycle is timed from checkpoint event emission to the replacement agent receiving its third event
the wall-clock cost of the full cycle does not exceed the bound committed to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json under key "checkpoint_wall_clock_ms"

as a hard absolute ceiling independent of any baseline: the checkpoint cycle must complete within 30 seconds on any CI runner matching the runner_spec; this absolute ceiling gate is a separate CI failure from the baseline-regression gate (the baseline may drift QoQ but can never exceed 30 seconds)
if the absolute ceiling is exceeded, the CI failure message explicitly reads "checkpoint cycle exceeded absolute ceiling of 30 seconds" — distinguishable from the baseline-regression message

the router enforces a minimum inter-checkpoint interval: an agent cannot be checkpointed again until at least the interval committed to performance-baselines.json under key "checkpoint_min_interval_events" (minimum number of routed events per agent since last renewal) has elapsed
this minimum interval prevents checkpoint thrash where a slow agent triggers renewal on every other turn
the thrash-prevention interval is configurable, not hardcoded in the router

scenarios.name: Checkpoint cycle wall-clock cost is bounded and checkpoint thrash prevention is enforced

steps.text: the checkpoint renewal sequence for "tla-writer" is in progress: checkpoint event sent, checkpoint_response received, Stop-BusAgent called but Start-BusAgent has not yet returned
the heartbeat timer fires during this window
the heartbeat banner omits "tla-writer" entirely (it is neither alive nor newly spawned yet)
the heartbeat banner does not show the old session_id as alive
the heartbeat banner does not show the new session_id as alive until Start-BusAgent has completed and written the new row to agent_sessions
no race condition produces a banner showing both the old and new session_id simultaneously

scenarios.name: Heartbeat arriving during an in-progress checkpoint sequence does not reflect stale agent state

steps.text: the checkpoint threshold fires at ~80% of the model context window
the heartbeat fires every 10 seconds
the router is evaluating context usage and an agent's total_tokens climbs toward the checkpoint threshold
at least one heartbeat tick fires between the agent's last result event and the token usage reaching 80% (because typical turns take longer than 10 seconds)
a monitoring dead zone — a window longer than 10 seconds during which the agent is approaching 80% context but no heartbeat is visible — cannot exist in steady state
the heartbeat idle-time column provides a visible proxy for context-window pressure: an agent with low idle time is making fast progress and approaching the threshold faster

scenarios.name: Heartbeat cadence is tied to the 80-percent checkpoint threshold so no monitoring dead zone exists

steps.text: the router has been running for a long session and a single event thread has accumulated 1000 in_reply_to hops (one reply referencing another, forming a deep chain)
the router resolves the in_reply_to chain for correlation (e.g., to display a conversation graph)
the router limits chain traversal to the depth bound committed to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json (key: "in_reply_to_chain_max_depth")
if a chain exceeds the bound, the router logs a "[WARN]" entry identifying the chain root evt_id and the truncated depth
no unbounded recursion or linear scan of the full event_log is performed for chain resolution

scenarios.name: in_reply_to chain depth is bounded to prevent correlation-table memory growth

steps.text: the router is processing 50 events per second (aggregate throughput scope) across 16 alive agents
ground-truth composition is measured across a 30-second window for each individual agent
the aggregate ground-truth composition time per event (summed across all 16 per-agent compositions triggered by that event) does not exceed 50 milliseconds

the per-agent mean composition time is 50ms ÷ 16 agents = 3.1ms (this is a mean, not a per-agent ceiling — the metric is labeled "ground_truth_composition_mean_ms_per_agent" in performance-baselines.json)
the per-agent p95 composition time (not the mean) does not exceed the bound committed to performance-baselines.json under key "ground_truth_composition_p95_ms_per_agent"
the ground-truth composition does not perform unbounded SQLite scans: it queries only indexed columns with a row limit of at most 2× the current alive agent count
the ground-truth query plan is verified via EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN: no full-table scan is produced
the per-turn SQLite read count per agent does not exceed 2 reads per alive agent per cycle

scenarios.name: Aggregate ground-truth composition budget — per-agent mean is a mean, not a per-agent ceiling; distribution must be bounded

steps.text: the router validates envelopes at 50 events per second under sustained load
v9 OBJ-5 added a second per-envelope ACL lookup for the recipient side (to-side partition check)
the test measures the per-envelope ACL validation time before and after enabling recipient-side validation over 500 consecutive envelopes
the combined from+to ACL validation p95 does not exceed the bound committed to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json under key "type_sender_acl_combined_p95_ms"
the recipient-side ACL overhead (isolated) does not exceed the bound committed under key "type_sender_acl_to_side_overhead_p95_ms"
the ratio of combined-ACL-p95 to from-only-ACL-p95 does not exceed 2.0 (the to-side lookup must not double total ACL cost at p95)
both bounds are committed as separate baseline keys so regressions in either dimension are independently reportable

scenarios.name: TypeSenderACL recipient-side (to-side) check does not add more than 50% overhead to the existing from-side check (v9 OBJ-5 hot-path)

steps.text: the router is processing HandlerAdapterCompletes at high frequency (test scenario: 50 completions per second across 1 handler, simulating checkpoint-heavy load where agents respawn frequently)
the per-completion epoch-guard check time is measured over 500 consecutive HandlerAdapterCompletes transitions
the epoch-guard check p95 latency does not exceed the bound committed to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json under key "handler_epoch_guard_p95_ms"
the bail-out rate (completions rejected by the epoch guard) under checkpoint-heavy load does not exceed the bound committed under key "handler_epoch_guard_max_rejection_rate_per_sec"
the epoch guard uses an O(1) lookup (not a scan of event_log) to compare spawnedAtEvt[a] against handlerPendingEpoch[h]
the test measures bail-out rate independently of overall HandlerAdapterCompletes throughput so stale-epoch spikes are visible without being masked by the passing-epoch baseline

scenarios.name: Handler-epoch guard (handlerPendingEpoch check on HandlerAdapterCompletes) does not exceed p95 latency budget under checkpoint-heavy load

steps.text: the bus is running with rollbackRequested=TRUE when a SQLite error occurs during rollback
RouterHaltsRollbackSqliteError fires and the router transitions to busStatus="halted"

the elapsed time from SQLite error detection to haltReason="mechanical_error" written to the final SQLite state does not exceed the bound committed to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json under key "rollback_sqlite_error_halt_latency_ms"
the bound is an absolute ceiling independent of any baseline: if the bound is exceeded, the CI failure message explicitly reads "RouterHaltsRollbackSqliteError halt latency exceeded bound"
rollbackRequested and rollbackTargetWorktree are cleared atomically in the same action as the halt transition (no partial state window)
the ConsensusRound aggregate reset (consensusState, unresolvedObjections, overriddenObjections, consensusRoundStart) is included in the same atomic write as the halt state — no partial reset window

scenarios.name: RouterHaltsRollbackSqliteError detection-to-halt latency is bounded

steps.text: the bus is in busStatus="halted" with rollbackRequested=TRUE (either via Ctrl+C or halted-state /rollback)
RouterAbortsStaleRollback fires and clears rollbackRequested to FALSE and rollbackTargetWorktree to NULL

the elapsed time from WF-fairness enabling condition satisfied to rollbackRequested=FALSE persisted to SQLite does not exceed the bound committed to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json under key "rollback_abort_stale_latency_ms"
the bound is symmetric with "rollback_sqlite_error_halt_latency_ms" (same order of magnitude — both measure rollback-subsystem state transitions under halt conditions)
the CI failure message explicitly reads "RouterAbortsStaleRollback clear latency exceeded bound" if breached
this baseline key is committed separately from the halt-latency key so regressions in the abort path are independently reportable

scenarios.name: RouterAbortsStaleRollback detection-to-clear latency is bounded

steps.text: the router is processing a debate with event_log containing 10000 rows (approximately 3.3 hours of pipeline activity)
consensusRoundStart=9500 (the last rollback advanced the epoch to evt_id 9500, leaving 9499 pre-epoch rows)
ModeratorEmitsCandidate fires and evaluates both the ∃ guard (any objection with evt_id >= 9500) and the ∀ ground-truth guard (all agents with objection evt_id >= 9500 have groundTruthDelivered=TRUE)
the guard evaluation p95 latency does not exceed the bound committed to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json under key "moderator_emits_candidate_guard_p95_ms"
EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN on the event_log query for the ∃ and ∀ guards shows no full-table scan (no "SCAN TABLE event_log" without an index on evt_id or type)
the guard uses the index on (type, evt_id) to filter "objection" rows with evt_id >= consensusRoundStart without scanning pre-epoch rows

the p95 budget is committed to performance-baselines.json under key "moderator_emits_candidate_guard_p95_ms" with an associated baseline key "moderator_emits_candidate_guard_baseline_rows" recording the eventLog row count at baseline capture time
the CI job fails if the guard evaluation increases p95 by more than 20% versus the baseline for the same eventLog row count

scenarios.name: ModeratorEmitsCandidate epoch-scoped eventLog scan p95 budget does not regress under large eventLog

steps.text: the router dispatches a debate round to 8 reviewers generating 2 events each (16 evt_id allocations in a burst within a single sub-second window)
the allocator is called 16 times in rapid succession during the fan-out burst
all 16 allocations complete within 200 milliseconds (burst window)
the burst throughput (16 allocations ÷ window) exceeds the sustained aggregate rate floor of 50 evt/s

the per-caller throughput floor under the burst is ≥ 31 allocations/second (500 allocator/s ÷ 16 callers = 31.25/caller as documented in performance-baselines.json under key "evt_id_allocator_per_caller_floor_allocs_per_sec")
the burst-envelope test is run separately from the sustained-throughput test and is committed as a distinct baseline key "evt_id_allocator_burst_16_wall_clock_ms"

scenarios.name: evt_id allocator burst envelope accounts for fan-out peaks exceeding aggregate throughput floor

steps.text: the TLA+ model uses MaxEvtId=4 (as configured in BidirectionalComms.cfg) and production uses Int64.MaxValue
the production router's evt_id allocator approaches Int64.MaxValue-1
the router detects the boundary condition (nextEvtId > Int64.MaxValue - 1) and halts with failureCategory="evt_id_overflow" and exit code 16

the RouterHaltsBoundReached action in BidirectionalComms.tla v10 (which fires at nextEvtId > MaxEvtId) is structurally identical at MaxEvtId=4 and Int64.MaxValue — the guard logic is parameterized, not hardcoded

a test verifies the boundary condition is exercised in the property test suite using MaxEvtId=4 (the model constant), proving the production guard is covered without requiring a 9.2×10^18 event sequence
tests/bus/performance-baselines.json documents this gap under key "evt_id_model_max" (value: 4) and "evt_id_production_max" (value: 9223372036854775807) for provenance

scenarios.name: MaxEvtId model-to-production gap is cross-wired — evt_id overflow halt (exit 16) maps to TLA+ MaxEvtId model boundary

features.name: The bus meets throughput, latency, and resource bounds required for the pipeline

steps.text: Stage 7 processes 10 done events across 4 tasks
git log is inspected on the feature branch
there are at most 10 commits carrying Vibe-Event-Id trailers
each Vibe-Event-Id value is unique in git history

scenarios.name: Each done event produces exactly one commit with a Vibe-Event-Id trailer

steps.text: the feature branch has multiple micro-commits from Stage 7
the branch is squashed before PR creation
the squashed commit message is inspected
it includes all unique Vibe-Event-Id values from the squashed commits
it includes all unique Vibe-Group-Id values from the squashed commits

scenarios.name: Vibe-Event-Id and Vibe-Group-Id trailers are present in git log after squash

steps.text: a pipeline crashed and is resumed via -Resume
event_log contains events evt-001 through evt-015 with status="committed"
the resumed pipeline runs
git log shows no new commits carrying Vibe-Event-Id values from evt-001 through evt-015
only events with status other than "committed" produce new commits

scenarios.name: Resume does not produce duplicate commits for already-committed events

steps.text: the CI job "vibe-event-id-global-uniqueness" runs on every pull request and merge to main
the job scans for Vibe-Event-Id trailers
the scan is bounded to commits reachable from the current HEAD within the last 90 days using "git log --since=90.days.ago --all --format=%B" to prevent an unbounded O(repo-history) scan on every PR
the job explicitly does NOT use an unbounded "git log --all" without a date or count scope limit
all commit messages containing "Vibe-Event-Id:" trailers within the scoped window are extracted
no Vibe-Event-Id value appears more than once across the extracted set
the CI job fails if any Vibe-Event-Id value is duplicated in the scoped history

the evt_id allocation strategy (SQLite AUTOINCREMENT scoped to the pipeline run) makes global collisions structurally impossible; the CI check is a defensive regression gate scoped to a reasonable history window

scenarios.name: Vibe-Event-Id values are globally unique across recent repository git history with a bounded scan

steps.text: an agent emits a done event for task "task-07" that is committed to git by the commit serializer
the commit serializer updates event_log status to "committed" for that done event
New-BusSnapshot is called automatically after the status update

New-BusSnapshot inserts one row into the bus_snapshots table with fields: snap_id (UUID v4), feature_name, git_sha (the commit SHA produced by the done event), last_committed_evt_id (the committed evt_id), and taken_at (UTC ISO 8601)
the bus_snapshots table is separate from event_log and is not governed by the append-only trigger
the snap_id is globally unique across all rows in bus_snapshots

scenarios.name: New-BusSnapshot creates a snapshot row after each successfully committed done event

steps.text: a done event commits successfully (status="committed") and another done event fails (status="routed", commit failed)
the commit serializer processes both events
New-BusSnapshot is called exactly once — for the committed event only
no snapshot row is created for the failed event
no snapshot row is created for events with status="routed", "rejected", or "delivery_failed"

scenarios.name: New-BusSnapshot is triggered once per successfully committed done event and not on failed or rolled-back events

steps.text: the bus_snapshots retention limit is configured at 10 snapshots per feature (default, not hardcoded)
bus_snapshots already contains 10 rows for feature "auth-flow"
New-BusSnapshot is called for the 11th committed done event for "auth-flow"
the oldest snapshot row for "auth-flow" (the row with the smallest snap_id or earliest taken_at) is deleted before the new row is inserted
bus_snapshots never contains more than the configured retention limit of rows per feature
the retention limit is stored in vibe configuration, not hardcoded in New-BusSnapshot

scenarios.name: Snapshot retention policy removes old snapshots beyond the configured limit

steps.text: the commit serializer holds the per-worktree mutex and calls New-BusSnapshot synchronously after each done commit
the test measures total commit-serializer wall-clock time for 20 consecutive done commits (baseline: serializer-only without New-BusSnapshot) and with New-BusSnapshot enabled
New-BusSnapshot completes its SQLite INSERT plus UUID-v4 generation and optional eviction DELETE
the p95 New-BusSnapshot wall-clock cost per commit does not exceed the bound committed to tests/bus/performance-baselines.json under key "new_bus_snapshot_p95_ms"
enabling New-BusSnapshot does not increase the commit-serializer p95 tail latency by more than 20% over the baseline commit-only measurement
this 20% budget is the same regression gate used for the commit-serializer itself — no special budget expansion for snapshot cost
the CI job fails if the New-BusSnapshot cost exceeds the baseline-relative cap, reporting "New-BusSnapshot inflated commit tail latency by X% (limit 20%)"

scenarios.name: New-BusSnapshot p95 cost does not inflate commit-serializer tail latency above baseline

steps.text: the repository is in a state where bus_snapshots contains at least one snapshot for feature "auth-flow"
the user invokes "./vibe.ps1 -Rollback -SnapId snap-003"
vibe.ps1 treats -Rollback as a distinct switch parameter (not -Resume, not -Status) that initiates the rollback workflow
-SnapId is a mandatory companion parameter to -Rollback identifying the target snapshot by its snap_id UUID
providing -Rollback without -SnapId produces an error message "Missing -SnapId: specify the target snapshot ID" and exits non-zero
providing -SnapId without -Rollback is treated as a parameter error and exits non-zero
the parameter contract is: ./vibe.ps1 -Rollback -SnapId <uuid> (no positional seed argument, no -Resume, no -Status may be combined with -Rollback)
the parameter block in vibe.ps1 formally declares -Rollback as a [switch] and -SnapId as a [string] with ParameterSetName="Rollback"

scenarios.name: /rollback CLI surface — vibe.ps1 accepts -Rollback -SnapId <snap_id> as a named parameter

steps.text: a pipeline is running (busStatus="running") for feature "auth-flow" with the commit lock idle (all commitLockHolder[wt] = null)
bus_snapshots contains a row snap-003 with git_sha="abc123" and last_committed_evt_id="evt-042"
the user invokes "./vibe.ps1 -Rollback -SnapId snap-003" (which internally sets rollbackRequested=TRUE)
the bus waits for all in-flight commits to drain (commits that were already in-progress complete; no new commits are accepted after rollbackRequested=TRUE is observed)
the bus calls Stop-AllBusAgents once commitLockHolder[wt] = null for all worktrees
the feature branch for "auth-flow" is reset to the git commit SHA "abc123" recorded in snap-003 (git reset --hard abc123 on the feature branch)

the router resets its in-memory state: committedDoneEvts is scoped to events with evt_id <= "evt-042", commitLockHolder is cleared, pendingDoneEvt is cleared — consistent with TLA+ RouterExecutesRollback action

event_log is NOT mutated — no rows are marked status="rolled_back" and no new "rollback_requested" event is appended (event_log is append-only and does not contain a "rollback_requested" type in the 16-value closed enum)
agent_sessions rows created after the snapshot point have status="ended" with ended_at set to the rollback timestamp (agent-lifecycle mutation is permitted; event_log mutation is not)
the pipeline halts with haltReason="user_rollback" and exits with code 0
a subsequent "./vibe.ps1 -Resume" re-spawns agents from the snapshot's bootstrap + ground_truth derived from SQLite state as of snap-003

scenarios.name: /rollback command restores the feature branch to a prior snapshot and halts cleanly — event_log is NOT mutated

steps.text: bus_snapshots contains zero rows for feature "auth-flow" (no snapshot has been taken yet)
the user invokes "./vibe.ps1 -Rollback -SnapId snap-003"
the router detects that no snapshot exists matching snap_id="snap-003" in bus_snapshots
the rollback is rejected with an error message "Rollback rejected: snapshot snap-003 not found in bus_snapshots for feature auth-flow"
the pipeline does NOT halt or modify any state
the process exits non-zero
this behavior satisfies the RollbackRequiresSnapshot invariant (TLA+ v8 OBJ-10)

scenarios.name: /rollback is rejected when no snapshot exists for the target worktree (RollbackRequiresSnapshot)

steps.text: the bus has already halted with haltReason="mechanical_error" and failureCategory="agent_crash" (busStatus="halted")
snapshotExists["wt-auth-flow"]=TRUE
the user invokes "./vibe.ps1 -Rollback -SnapId snap-003" (UserRequestsRollback fires, setting rollbackRequested=TRUE)
RouterExecutesRollback does NOT fire because busStatus is not "running" (BusRunning guard required)
RouterAbortsStaleRollback fires (v11 OBJ-C: busStatus="halted" ∧ rollbackRequested=TRUE) and clears rollbackRequested to FALSE and rollbackTargetWorktree to NULL
snapshotExists["wt-auth-flow"] remains TRUE — snapshot preserved for retry after -Resume
busStatus="halted", haltReason="mechanical_error", failureCategory="agent_crash" are all preserved unchanged (RouterAbortsStaleRollback does NOT modify these)
consensusRoundStart is NOT advanced (RouterAbortsStaleRollback does not advance the epoch — no clean-slate boundary occurred)

steps.text: the bus halts for different FailureCategory values

agent_crash exits with code 15

no two FailureCategories share an exit code (there are 7 distinct non-zero codes for 7 FailureCategories)

RouterAbortsStaleRollback fires post-halt and does NOT change the exit code — the bus is already halted (exit code set by the original halt action: exit 1 for user_interrupt, or 10-16 for mechanical halt); RouterAbortsStaleRollback only clears rollbackRequested and rollbackTargetWorktree in-memory and has no exit-code assignment of its own

steps.text: the event_log table has a SQLite trigger enforcing the append-only invariant
the trigger definition is inspected
the trigger raises an error on any DELETE against event_log (unconditionally)
the trigger raises an error on any UPDATE that changes a column other than "status"
the trigger raises an error on any UPDATE to status that is not in the whitelisted transition set
the whitelisted status transitions are exactly: "routed" → "committed", "routed" → "delivery_failed", "routed" → "rejected"

the transition "routed" → "rolled_back" does NOT exist — event_log rows are never marked "rolled_back" because rollback is a git-branch-level operation that does not mutate event_log (event_log is append-only; RouterExecutesRollback resets in-memory state only)
any UPDATE attempting "committed" → "routed" or any other non-whitelisted direction is rejected by the trigger
INSERT operations are always permitted (the append guarantee holds for new rows)

scenarios.name: The event_log append-only trigger permits only whitelisted status transitions via UPDATE

the trigger fires and the UPDATE is rejected with an error
event_log row for evt_id="evt-042" retains type="done" unchanged
the "append-only" claim in the glossary is accurate: rows cannot be modified except for the whitelisted status column transition

steps.text: an agent sends an envelope with type="protocol\u005Ferror" (which decodes to "protocol_error" after Unicode unescaping)
the router validates the type field
the router rejects the envelope with a protocol_error identifying the raw type value "protocol\u005Ferror" as non-conforming
the router enforces that the type field must be NFC-normalized printable ASCII with no Unicode escapes or non-ASCII characters
no envelope whose type field requires Unicode decoding to match a known type is ever accepted

steps.text: an agent sends an envelope with from="tla\u2010writer" (where \u2010 is the Unicode non-breaking hyphen, visually identical to ASCII hyphen in "tla-writer")
the router validates the from field
the router rejects the envelope with a protocol_error identifying the from field as containing non-ASCII characters (\u2010 is not ASCII)
a separate send with to="tla\u2010writer" (same Unicode hyphen in the to field) is likewise rejected before lookup
"tla\u2010writer" and "tla-writer" are treated as DISTINCT agents: the Unicode-hyphen form is always rejected, never auto-normalized to the ASCII form (normalization is a rejection, not a coercion)
the router does NOT silently apply NFC normalization to from/to values and proceed — it rejects any from or to field that contains non-ASCII characters with a protocol_error
this prevents a unicode-hyphen spoofed from address from impersonating a legitimate agent name in the router's agent_sessions lookup

scenarios.name: A post-deploy rollout health metric is captured, validated, and retained for comparison with subsequent deployments

steps.text: the current deployment has produced a metrics.json with total_run_duration_ms=45000 and total_events_routed=320
the prior successful deployment's metrics.json is retained in the pipeline state directory under the naming convention "metrics-<deploy_id>.json"
the CI post-deploy comparator job runs
it locates the most recent prior metrics.json (by deploy_id) from the same pipeline state directory
it compares total_run_duration_ms: a regression of more than 25% versus the prior deploy is a CI failure
it compares total_events_routed: a drop of more than 20% versus the prior deploy (for equivalent feature complexity) is a CI warning
the comparator reports results as a CI annotation distinguishing: "REGRESSION" (hard fail), "DEGRADED" (warning), "IMPROVED" (informational), "UNCHANGED" (pass)
if no prior metrics.json exists (first deploy), the comparator skips comparison and logs "no prior baseline for cross-deploy comparison — skipping"
the comparator job is a required post-deploy step for all production deployments; its absence is itself a CI failure

scenarios.name: metrics.json is compared against the prior successful deploy to catch cross-deploy regressions (the sole safety net absent canary/shadow modes)

features.name: Post-deployment observability provides the sole safety net in the absence of canary or shadow modes
